entries.title: The Aesop for Children: Aesop Passage A

paragraphs.text: The Tortoise, you know, carries his house on his back. No matter how hard he tries, he cannot leave home. They say that Jupiter punished him so, because he was such a lazy stay-at-home that he would not go to Jupiter's wedding, even when especially invited.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 你知道，乌龟背着他的房子。无论他如何努力，他都无法离开家。他们说朱庇特如此惩罚他，因为他是如此懒惰的宅家，以至于即使受到特别邀请，他也不会参加朱庇特的婚礼。

paragraphs.text: After many years, Tortoise began to wish he had gone to that wedding. When he saw how gaily the birds flew about and how the Hare and the Chipmunk and all the other animals ran nimbly by, always eager to see everything there was to be seen, the Tortoise felt very sad and discontented. He wanted to see the world too, and there he was with a house on his back and little short legs that could hardly drag him along.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 许多年后，乌龟开始希望自己没有去参加那场婚礼。当他看到鸟儿欢快地飞来飞去，野兔、花栗鼠和其他动物敏捷地跑过，总是渴望看到一切可看的东西时，乌龟感到非常悲伤和不满。他也想去看看这个世界，而他的背上背着一座房子，小短腿几乎拖不动他。

entries.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland: Alice Passage A

paragraphs.text: Poor Alice! It was as much as she could do, lying down on one side, to look through into the garden with one eye; but to get through was more hopeless than ever: she sat down and began to cry again.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 可怜的爱丽丝！她只能侧身躺下，用一只眼睛向花园里看去。但要度过难关比以往任何时候都更加无望：她坐下来，又开始哭泣。

paragraphs.text: "You ought to be ashamed of yourself," said Alice, "a great girl like you," (she might well say this), "to go on crying in this way! Stop this moment, I tell you!" But she went on all the same, shedding gallons of tears, until there was a large pool all round her, about four inches deep and reaching half down the hall.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你应该为自己感到羞耻，”爱丽丝说，“像你这样一个伟大的女孩，”（她很可能会这样说），“继续这样哭泣！我告诉你，现在就停下来！”但她还是继续说下去，流下了无数的眼泪，直到她周围出现一个大水池，大约四英寸深，延伸到大厅的一半。

entries.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland: Alice Passage B

paragraphs.text: Poor Alice! It was as much as she could do, lying down on one side, to look through into the garden with one eye; but to get through was more hopeless than ever: she sat down and began to cry again.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 可怜的爱丽丝！她只能侧身躺下，用一只眼睛向花园里看去。但要度过难关比以往任何时候都更加无望：她坐下来，又开始哭泣。

paragraphs.text: "You ought to be ashamed of yourself," said Alice, "a great girl like you," (she might well say this), "to go on crying in this way! Stop this moment, I tell you!" But she went on all the same, shedding gallons of tears, until there was a large pool all round her, about four inches deep and reaching half down the hall.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你应该为自己感到羞耻，”爱丽丝说，“像你这样一个伟大的女孩，”（她很可能会这样说），“继续这样哭泣！我告诉你，现在就停下来！”但她还是继续说下去，流下了无数的眼泪，直到她周围出现一个大水池，大约四英寸深，延伸到大厅的一半。

paragraphs.text: After a time she heard a little pattering of feet in the distance, and she hastily dried her eyes to see what was coming. It was the White Rabbit returning, splendidly dressed, with a pair of white kid gloves in one hand and a large fan in the other: he came trotting along in a great hurry, muttering to himself as he came, "Oh! the Duchess, the Duchess! Oh! won't she be savage if I've kept her waiting!" Alice felt so desperate that she was ready to ask help of any one; so, when the Rabbit came near her, she began, in a low, timid voice, "If you please, sir—" The Rabbit started violently, dropped the white kid gloves and the fan, and skurried away into the darkness as hard as he could go.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 过了一会儿，她听到远处传来轻微的脚步声，她赶紧擦干眼睛看看接下来会发生什么。原来是白兔回来了，衣冠楚楚，一手戴着白羊皮手套，一手拿着一把大扇，小跑着走来，一边走一边自言自语道：“哎哟！公爵夫人，公爵夫人！哦！如果我让她久等了，她会不会太野蛮了！”爱丽丝感到非常绝望，她准备向任何人寻求帮助。于是，当兔子走近她时，她用低沉、胆怯的声音开始说道：“先生，请您——”兔子猛地一惊，丢下白羊皮手套和扇子，拼命地跑进黑暗中。

entries.title: The Secret Garden: Secret Garden Passage A

paragraphs.text: But Mrs. Medlock was not in the least disturbed by her and her thoughts. She was the kind of woman who would "stand no nonsense from young ones." At least, that is what she would have said if she had been asked. She had not wanted to go to London just when her sister Maria's daughter was going to be married, but she had a comfortable, well paid place as housekeeper at Misselthwaite Manor and the only way in which she could keep it was to do at once what Mr. Archibald Craven told her to do. She never dared even to ask a question.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 但梅德劳克太太丝毫没有被她和她的想法所困扰。她是那种“不能忍受年轻人胡言乱语”的女人。至少，如果有人问她的话，她会这么说。当她姐姐玛丽亚的女儿即将结婚时，她不想去伦敦，但她在米瑟斯韦特庄园有一个舒适、报酬丰厚的管家职位，而她能保住这个职位的唯一方法就是立即按照阿奇博尔德·克雷文先生的吩咐去做。她连问题都不敢问。

paragraphs.text: "Captain Lennox and his wife died of the cholera," Mr. Craven had said in his short, cold way. "Captain Lennox was my wife's brother and I am their daughter's guardian. The child is to be brought here. You must go to London and bring her yourself."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “伦诺克斯船长和他的妻子死于霍乱，”克雷文先生用他简短而冷酷的语气说道。“伦诺克斯船长是我妻子的兄弟，我是他们女儿的监护人。孩子要被带到这里来。你必须亲自去伦敦带她来。”

paragraphs.text: Mary sat in her corner of the railway carriage and looked plain and fretful. She had nothing to read or to look at, and she had folded her thin little black-gloved hands in her lap. Her black dress made her look yellower than ever, and her limp light hair straggled from under her black crêpe hat.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 玛丽坐在火车车厢的角落里，看上去平淡而焦躁。她没有什么可读或可看的，她把戴着黑手套的瘦小手交叠在腿上。她的黑色连衣裙使她看起来比以前更黄，柔软的浅色头发从黑色绉纱帽下散落下来。

entries.title: The Secret Garden: Secret Garden Passage B

paragraphs.text: For a moment Basil looked angry, and then he began to tease. He was always teasing his sisters. He danced round and round her and made faces and sang and laughed.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 巴兹尔一时显得很生气，然后他开始取笑。他总是捉弄他的姐妹们。他围着她跳舞、做鬼脸、唱歌、大笑。

paragraphs.text: "She doesn't know where home is!" said Basil, with seven-year-old scorn. "It's England, of course. Our grandmama lives there and our sister Mabel was sent to her last year. You are not going to your grandmama. You have none. You are going to your uncle. His name is Mr. Archibald Craven."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “她不知道家在哪里！”巴兹尔带着七岁孩子的轻蔑说道。“当然是英格兰。我们的祖母住在那里，我们的妹妹梅布尔去年被送到她那里。你不会去你奶奶那里。你没有。你要去你叔叔那里。他的名字是阿奇博尔德·克雷文先生。”

paragraphs.text: "I know you don't," Basil answered. "You don't know anything. Girls never do. I heard father and mother talking about him. He lives in a great, big, desolate old house in the country and no one goes near him. He's so cross he won't let them, and they wouldn't come if he would let them. He's a hunchback, and he's horrid."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “我知道你不知道，”巴兹尔回答。“你什么都不知道。女孩子从来不这样。我听到爸爸和妈妈谈论他。他住在乡下一栋又大又大又荒凉的老房子里，没有人靠近他。他太生气了，他不让他们来，即使他让他们来，他们也不会来。他是个驼背，而且很可怕。”

paragraphs.text: But she thought over it a great deal afterward; and when Mrs. Crawford told her that night that she was going to sail away to England in a few days and go to her uncle, Mr. Archibald Craven, who lived at Misselthwaite Manor, she looked so stony and stubbornly uninterested that they did not know what to think about her. They tried to be kind to her, but she only turned her face away when Mrs. Crawford attempted to kiss her, and held herself stiffly when Mr. Crawford patted her shoulder.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 但事后她想了很多；那天晚上，当克劳福德夫人告诉她，几天后她将乘船前往英国，去找她住在米瑟斯韦特庄园的叔叔阿奇博尔德·克雷文先生时，她看上去那么冷漠、顽固地漠不关心，以至于他们不知道该怎么看待她。他们试图对她友善，但当克劳福德夫人试图亲吻她时，她只是把脸转开，当克劳福德先生拍拍她的肩膀时，她僵硬地保持着自己的姿势。

paragraphs.text: "She is such a plain child," Mrs. Crawford said pityingly, afterward. "And her mother was such a pretty creature. She had a very pretty manner, too, and Mary has the most unattractive ways I ever saw in a child. The children call her 'Mistress Mary Quite Contrary,' and though it's naughty of them, one can't help understanding it."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “她真是个平凡的孩子，”克劳福德夫人事后怜悯地说。“她的母亲是一个非常漂亮的人。她的举止也非常漂亮，而玛丽的举止是我在孩子身上见过的最没有吸引力的。孩子们称她为‘完全相反的玛丽小姐’，虽然这很顽皮，但人们还是不能不理解。”

paragraphs.text: "Perhaps if her mother had carried her pretty face and her pretty manners oftener into the nursery Mary might have learned some pretty ways too. It is very sad, now the poor beautiful thing is gone, to remember that many people never even knew that she had a child at all."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “如果她妈妈经常把她漂亮的脸蛋和漂亮的举止带进育儿室，也许玛丽也能学到一些漂亮的方法。令人非常难过的是，现在这个可怜的美丽的东西已经消失了，要记住很多人甚至根本不知道她有一个孩子。”

paragraphs.text: "I believe she scarcely ever looked at her," sighed Mrs. Crawford. "When her Ayah was dead there was no one to give a thought to the little thing. Think of the servants running away and leaving her all alone in that deserted bungalow. Colonel McGrew said he nearly jumped out of his skin when he opened the door and found her standing by herself in the middle of the room."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “我相信她几乎没有看她一眼，”克劳福德夫人叹了口气。“当她的阿亚死后，没有人会想到这个小东西。想象一下仆人们逃跑了，把她独自留在那间废弃的平房里。麦克格鲁上校说，当他打开门并发现她独自站在房间中央时，他差点惊慌失措。”

paragraphs.text: Mary made the long voyage to England under the care of an officer's wife, who was taking her children to leave them in a boarding-school. She was very much absorbed in her own little boy and girl, and was rather glad to hand the child over to the woman Mr. Archibald Craven sent to meet her, in London. The woman was his housekeeper at Misselthwaite Manor, and her name was Mrs. Medlock. She was a stout woman, with very red cheeks and sharp black eyes. She wore a very purple dress, a black silk mantle with jet fringe on it and a black bonnet with purple velvet flowers which stuck up and trembled when she moved her head. Mary did not like her at all, but as she very seldom liked people there was nothing remarkable in that; besides which it was very evident Mrs. Medlock did not think much of her.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 玛丽在一位军官的妻子的照顾下长途航行到了英国，她带着她的孩子们把他们留在了寄宿学校。她非常专注于自己的小男孩和小女孩，并很高兴将孩子交给阿奇博尔德·克雷文先生派来伦敦接她的女人。这个女人是他在米瑟斯韦特庄园的管家，她的名字叫莫德洛克夫人。她是个身材矮胖的女人，脸颊通红，黑眼睛锐利。她穿着一件非常紫色的衣服，一件黑色的丝绸斗篷，上面有黑色的流苏，戴着一顶黑色的帽子，帽子上有紫色的天鹅绒花朵，当她移动头部时，帽子会竖起来并颤抖。玛丽根本不喜欢她，但由于她很少喜欢别人，所以这没什么了不起的。除此之外，很明显梅德洛克夫人对她评价不高。

entries.title: Anne of Green Gables: Anne Passage A

paragraphs.text: Yet Matthew wished to keep her, of all unaccountable things! Marilla felt that he wanted it just as much this morning as he had the night before, and that he would go on wanting it. That was Matthew's way--take a whim into his head and cling to it with the most amazing silent persistency--a persistency ten times more potent and effectual in its very silence than if he had talked it out.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 然而马修却想留下她，这是最不负责任的事情！马瑞拉觉得他今天早上和前一天晚上一样想要它，而且他会继续想要它。这就是马修的方式——脑子里突发奇想，然后以最惊人的沉默坚持不懈——这种坚持在沉默中比他把它说出来要强大和有效十倍。

paragraphs.text: "Pretty well. I'm better at looking after children, though. I've had so much experience at that. It's such a pity you haven't any here for me to look after."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “很好。不过我更擅长照顾孩子。我在这方面有很多经验。可惜你这里没有人可以让我照顾。”

paragraphs.text: "I think he's lovely," said Anne reproachfully. "He is so very sympathetic. He didn't mind how much I talked--he seemed to like it. I felt that he was a kindred spirit as soon as ever I saw him."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “我觉得他很可爱。”安妮责备地说。“他非常富有同情心。他并不介意我说了多少——他似乎很喜欢。我一见到他就觉得他是志同道合的人。”

paragraphs.text: "You're both queer enough, if that's what you mean by kindred spirits," said Marilla with a sniff. "Yes, you may wash the dishes. Take plenty of hot water, and be sure you dry them well. I've got enough to attend to this morning for I'll have to drive over to White Sands in the afternoon and see Mrs. Spencer. You'll come with me and we'll settle what's to be done with you. After you've finished the dishes go up-stairs and make your bed."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你们俩都够奇怪的了，如果这就是你们所说的志趣相投的话。”玛丽拉嗤之以鼻地说。“是的，你可以洗碗。准备充足的热水，并确保将它们充分擦干。今天早上我有足够的事情要做，因为下午我必须开车去白沙去见斯宾塞夫人。你跟我来，我们会商量如何处置你。洗完了碗，就上楼去整理床铺吧。”

paragraphs.text: Anne washed the dishes deftly enough, as Marilla, who kept a sharp eye on the process, discerned. Later on she made her bed less successfully, for she had never learned the art of wrestling with a feather tick. But it was done somehow and smoothed down; and then Marilla, to get rid of her, told her she might go out-of-doors and amuse herself until dinnertime.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 安妮洗碗的动作非常熟练，玛丽拉敏锐地观察着整个过程，她发现了这一点。后来她整理床铺就不那么成功了，因为她从来没有学过与羽毛蜱摔跤的艺术。但不知何故，事情已经解决了，并且平息下来了。然后玛丽拉为了摆脱她，告诉她可以出去玩直到晚餐时间。

paragraphs.text: Anne flew to the door, face alight, eyes glowing. On the very threshold she stopped short, wheeled about, came back and sat down by the table, light and glow as effectually blotted out as if some one had clapped an extinguisher on her.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 安妮飞奔到门口，脸上容光焕发，眼睛闪闪发光。就在门口，她突然停了下来，转了个身，回来坐在桌边，光线和光芒实际上被遮蔽了，就像有人把灭火器拍到了她身上一样。

paragraphs.text: "Oh, I don't mean that sort of a name. I mean just a name you gave it yourself. Didn't you give it a name? May I give it one then? May I call it--let me see--Bonny would do--may I call it Bonny while I'm here? Oh, do let me!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “哦，我不是指那种名字。我的意思只是你自己给它起的名字。你没给它起名字吗？那我可以给它一个吗？我可以叫它——让我想想——邦尼会吗——我在这儿的时候可以叫它邦尼吗？噢，让我来吧！”

paragraphs.text: "Oh, I like things to have handles even if they are only geraniums. It makes them seem more like people. How do you know but that it hurts a geranium's feelings just to be called a geranium and nothing else? You wouldn't like to be called nothing but a woman all the time. Yes, I shall call it Bonny. I named that cherry-tree outside my bedroom window this morning. I called it Snow Queen because it was so white. Of course, it won't always be in blossom, but one can imagine that it is, can't one?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “哦，我喜欢有把手的东西，即使它们只是天竺葵。这让他们看起来更像人。你怎么知道仅仅被称为天竺葵而不是别的就伤害了天竺葵的感情？你不希望一直被称为女人。是的，我会叫它邦尼。今天早上我给卧室窗外的那棵樱桃树起了名字。我称它为雪女王，因为它太白了。当然，它不会永远盛开，但人们可以想象它是盛开的，不是吗？”

paragraphs.text: Anne had relapsed into reverie, with her chin in her hands and her eyes on the sky, when Marilla returned from her cellar pilgrimage. There Marilla left her until the early dinner was on the table.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 当玛丽拉从地窖朝圣回来时，安妮双手托着下巴，眼睛望着天空，再次陷入沉思。玛丽拉离开了她，直到早饭上桌。

paragraphs.text: "I'm going to drive over to White Sands and settle this thing. I'll take Anne with me and Mrs. Spencer will probably make arrangements to send her back to Nova Scotia at once. I'll set your tea out for you and I'll be home in time to milk the cows."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “我要开车去白沙解决这件事。我会带安妮一起去，斯宾塞夫人可能会立即安排将她送回新斯科舍省。我会为你准备茶，然后我会及时回家挤牛奶。”

entries.title: Little Women: Little Women Passage A

paragraphs.text: "You don't have half such a hard time as I do," said Jo. "How would you like to be shut up for hours with a nervous, fussy old lady, who keeps you trotting, is never satisfied, and worries you till you're ready to fly out the window or cry?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你的困难没有我一半多，”乔说。“你愿意和一个紧张、挑剔的老太太关上几个小时吗？她让你不停地小跑，永远不会满足，还担心你，直到你准备飞出窗外或哭泣？”

paragraphs.text: "It's naughty to fret, but I do think washing dishes and keeping things tidy is the worst work in the world. It makes me cross, and my hands get so stiff, I can't practice well at all." And Beth looked at her rough hands with a sigh that any one could hear that time.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “担心是很顽皮的，但我确实认为洗碗和保持东西整洁是世界上最糟糕的工作。这让我很生气，我的手变得很僵硬，根本练不好。”贝丝看着她粗糙的双手，叹了口气，当时任何人都能听到。

entries.title: Around the World in Eighty Days: Around the World Passage A

paragraphs.text: A memorandum of the wager was at once drawn up and signed by the six parties, during which Phileas Fogg preserved a stoical composure. He certainly did not bet to win, and had only staked the twenty thousand pounds, half of his fortune, because he foresaw that he might have to expend the other half to carry out this difficult, not to say unattainable, project. As for his antagonists, they seemed much agitated; not so much by the value of their stake, as because they had some scruples about betting under conditions so difficult to their friend.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 六方立即起草并签署了一份打赌备忘录，期间菲利斯·福格保持着坚忍的镇静。他当然不是赌赢，只赌了两万英镑，即他一半的财产，因为他预见到他可能需要花费另一半来完成这个困难的，甚至可以说是遥不可及的项目。至于他的对手，他们似乎很激动。与其说是因为他们的赌注价值，不如说是因为他们对在对他们的朋友来说如此困难的条件下下注有一些顾虑。

entries.title: Anne of Green Gables: Anne Passage B

paragraphs.text: Matthew, much to his own surprise, was enjoying himself. Like most quiet folks he liked talkative people when they were willing to do the talking themselves and did not expect him to keep up his end of it. But he had never expected to enjoy the society of a little girl. Women were bad enough in all conscience, but little girls were worse. He detested the way they had of sidling past him timidly, with sidewise glances, as if they expected him to gobble them up at a mouthful if they ventured to say a word. That was the Avonlea type of well-bred little girl. But this freckled witch was very different, and although he found it rather difficult for his slower intelligence to keep up with her brisk mental processes he thought that he "kind of liked her chatter."
paragraphs.translation_zh: 令马修自己惊讶的是，他玩得很开心。像大多数安静的人一样，他喜欢健谈的人，因为他们愿意自己说话，并且不希望他继续说下去。但他没想到会喜欢和一个小女孩交往。凭良心来说，女人已经够糟糕的了，但小女孩更糟糕。他讨厌他们胆怯地从他身边侧过，斜视着，仿佛他们期待着如果他们敢说一句话，他就会把他们一口吞掉。那是埃文利类型的有教养的小女孩。但这个长着雀斑的女巫却很不一样，尽管他发现自己较慢的智力很难跟上她敏捷的思维过程，但他认为自己“有点喜欢她的喋喋不休”。

paragraphs.text: "Oh, I'm so glad. I know you and I are going to get along together fine. It's such a relief to talk when one wants to and not be told that children should be seen and not heard. I've had that said to me a million times if I have once. And people laugh at me because I use big words. But if you have big ideas you have to use big words to express them, haven't you?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “哦，我很高兴。我知道你和我会相处得很好。当一个人想说话的时候就可以说话，而不是被告知孩子应该被看到而不是被听到，这真是一种解脱。这句话我已经听过一百万次了（如果有的话）。人们嘲笑我，因为我用了大词。但如果你有伟大的想法，你就必须用伟大的词语来表达它们，不是吗？”

paragraphs.text: "Fancy. It's always been one of my dreams to live near a brook. I never expected I would, though. Dreams don't often come true, do they? Wouldn't it be nice if they did? But just now I feel pretty nearly perfectly happy. I can't feel exactly perfectly happy because--well, what color would you call this?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “想要。住在小溪附近一直是我的梦想之一。但我从没想过我会这么做。梦想通常不会成真，不是吗？如果他们这样做不是很好吗？但现在我感觉几乎非常幸福。我感觉不到完全的快乐，因为——好吧，你觉得这是什么颜色？”

entries.title: Little Women: Little Women Passage B

paragraphs.text: "Glad to find you so merry, my girls," said a cheery voice at the door, and actors and audience turned to welcome a tall, motherly lady with a 'can I help you' look about her which was truly delightful. She was not elegantly dressed, but a noble-looking woman, and the girls thought the gray cloak and unfashionable bonnet covered the most splendid mother in the world.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “很高兴看到你们如此快乐，我的女儿们，”门口传来一个欢快的声音，演员和观众转过身来欢迎一位身材高大、慈母般的女士，她的表情是“我可以帮你吗”，这真是令人愉快。她的衣着并不华丽，但却是一位相貌高贵的女人，女孩们认为灰色的斗篷和不合时宜的帽子掩盖了世界上最辉煌的母亲。

paragraphs.text: "Well, dearies, how have you got on today? There was so much to do, getting the boxes ready to go tomorrow, that I didn't come home to dinner. Has anyone called, Beth? How is your cold, Meg? Jo, you look tired to death. Come and kiss me, baby."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “嗯，亲爱的，今天过得怎么样？有太多事情要做，要准备好明天要走的箱子，所以我没有回家吃晚饭。贝丝，有人打电话来吗？梅格，你感冒怎么样？乔，你看起来累得要死。过来吻我吧，宝贝。”

paragraphs.text: While making these maternal inquiries Mrs. March got her wet things off, her warm slippers on, and sitting down in the easy chair, drew Amy to her lap, preparing to enjoy the happiest hour of her busy day. The girls flew about, trying to make things comfortable, each in her own way. Meg arranged the tea table, Jo brought wood and set chairs, dropping, over-turning, and clattering everything she touched. Beth trotted to and fro between parlor kitchen, quiet and busy, while Amy gave directions to everyone, as she sat with her hands folded.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 马奇夫人一边做着这些母亲般的询问，一边脱掉湿漉漉的衣服，穿上温暖的拖鞋，坐在安乐椅上，把艾米拉到腿上，准备享受忙碌一天中最快乐的时光。女孩们飞来飞去，试图以自己的方式让一切变得舒适。梅格摆好茶几，乔拿来木头和椅子，她碰过的所有东西都掉落、翻倒、叮当作响。贝丝在客厅厨房之间小跑着，安静而忙碌，而艾米则双手交叉坐着，给每个人指路。

entries.title: Around the World in Eighty Days: Around the World Passage B

paragraphs.text: "A true Englishman doesn't joke when he is talking about so serious a thing as a wager," replied Phileas Fogg, solemnly. "I will bet twenty thousand pounds against anyone who wishes that I will make the tour of the world in eighty days or less; in nineteen hundred and twenty hours, or a hundred and fifteen thousand two hundred minutes. Do you accept?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “一个真正的英国人在谈论打赌这样严肃的事情时是不会开玩笑的，”菲利斯·福格严肃地回答道。“如果有人希望我能在八十天或更短的时间内、在一千九百二十小时内，或者在十一万五千二百分钟内环游世界，我将与任何人打赌两万英镑。你接受吗？”

paragraphs.text: "This very evening," returned Phileas Fogg. He took out and consulted a pocket almanac, and added, "As today is Wednesday, the 2nd of October, I shall be due in London in this very room of the Reform Club, on Saturday, the 21st of December, at a quarter before nine p. m. ; or else the twenty thousand pounds, now deposited in my name at Baring's, will belong to you, in fact and in right, gentlemen. Here is a cheque for the amount."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “就在今天晚上，”菲利斯·福格回答道。他拿出一本袖珍年鉴，补充道：“今天是 10 月 2 日星期三，我将于 12 月 21 日星期六晚上 9 点差一刻抵达伦敦改革俱乐部的这个房间。米。;否则，现在以我的名义存入巴林银行的两万英镑将属于你们，事实上，先生们，这是正确的。这是一张金额的支票。”

paragraphs.text: A memorandum of the wager was at once drawn up and signed by the six parties, during which Phileas Fogg preserved a stoical composure. He certainly did not bet to win, and had only staked the twenty thousand pounds, half of his fortune, because he foresaw that he might have to expend the other half to carry out this difficult, not to say unattainable, project. As for his antagonists, they seemed much agitated; not so much by the value of their stake, as because they had some scruples about betting under conditions so difficult to their friend.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 六方立即起草并签署了一份打赌备忘录，期间菲利斯·福格保持着坚忍的镇静。他当然不是赌赢，只赌了两万英镑，即他一半的财产，因为他预见到他可能需要花费另一半来完成这个困难的，甚至可以说是遥不可及的项目。至于他的对手，他们似乎很激动。与其说是因为他们的赌注价值，不如说是因为他们对在对他们的朋友来说如此困难的条件下下注有一些顾虑。

paragraphs.text: "We'll have no trunks; only a carpet-bag, with two shirts and three pairs of stockings for me, and the same for you. We'll buy our clothes on the way. Bring down my mackintosh and traveling-cloak, and some stout shoes, though we shall do little walking. Make haste!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “我们没有行李箱；只有一个地毯袋，我有两件衬衫和三双袜子，你也一样。我们会在路上买衣服。带上我的雨衣和旅行斗篷，还有一些结实的鞋子，尽管我们很少走路。赶快！”

paragraphs.text: Passepartout tried to reply, but could not. He went out, mounted to his own room, fell into a chair, and muttered: "That's good, that is! And I, who wanted to remain quiet!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: 路路通试图回复，但无法回复。他走出去，爬上自己的房间，一屁股坐在椅子上，嘀咕道：“那就好！而我，本想保持安静！”

paragraphs.text: He mechanically set about making the preparations for departure. Around the world in eighty days! Was his master a fool? No. Was this a joke, then? They were going to Dover; good! To Calais; good again! After all, Passepartout, who had been away from France five years, would not be sorry to set foot on his native soil again. Perhaps they would go as far as Paris, and it would do his eyes good to see Paris once more. But surely a gentleman so chary of his steps would stop there; no doubt—but, then, it was none the less true that he was going away, this so domestic person hitherto!
paragraphs.translation_zh: 他机械地开始准备出发。八十天环游世界！他的主人是个傻子吗？不。那么这是一个笑话吗？他们要去多佛；好的！前往加莱；又好了！毕竟，离开法国五年的路路通，再次踏上祖国的土地，不会感到遗憾。也许他们会一直走到巴黎，再次见到巴黎对他的眼睛会有好处。但一位如此小心谨慎的绅士肯定会就此止步。毫无疑问——但是，话虽如此，他还是要离开，这个迄今为止如此居家的人！

paragraphs.text: By eight o'clock Passepartout had packed the modest carpet-bag, containing the wardrobes of his master and himself; then, still troubled in mind, he carefully shut the door of his room, and descended to Mr. Fogg.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 到了八点钟，路路通已经收拾好了那个朴素的地毯袋，里面装着他主人和他自己的衣柜。然后，心里仍然不安，他小心翼翼地关上房门，下楼到福格先生身边。

entries.title: Treasure Island: Treasure Island Passage A

paragraphs.text: "That's you, Bill," returned Black Dog, "you're in the right of it, Billy. I'll have a glass of rum from this dear child here, as I've took such a liking to; and we'll sit down, if you please, and talk square, like old shipmates."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “就是你，比尔，”黑狗回答道，“你是对的，比利。我要喝一杯这个亲爱的孩子给我的朗姆酒，因为我非常喜欢；如果你愿意的话，我们可以坐下来，像老船友一样开诚布公地谈谈。”

paragraphs.text: He bade me go and leave the door wide open. "None of your keyholes for me, sonny," he said; and I left them together and retired into the bar.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 他吩咐我走，把门敞开。“儿子，你的钥匙孔不适合我，”他说。我把他们留在了一起，然后退进了酒吧。

paragraphs.text: Then all of a sudden there was a tremendous explosion of oaths and other noises--the chair and table went over in a lump, a clash of steel followed, and then a cry of pain, and the next instant I saw Black Dog in full flight, and the captain hotly pursuing, both with drawn cutlasses, and the former streaming blood from the left shoulder. Just at the door the captain aimed at the fugitive one last tremendous cut, which would certainly have split him to the chine had it not been intercepted by our big signboard of Admiral Benbow. You may see the notch on the lower side of the frame to this day.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 然后突然爆发出一阵巨大的咒骂声和其他声音——椅子和桌子一起倒了下去，接着是一声钢铁撞击声，然后是一声痛苦的叫声，下一瞬间我看到黑狗全速逃跑，而船长则紧追不舍，两人都拿着拔出的弯刀，前者的左肩流着血。就在门口，船长瞄准了逃亡者最后一击，如果不是我们的本博海军上将的大招牌拦截的话，这肯定会把他劈成脊椎骨。直到今天，您仍然可以在框架下侧看到凹口。

paragraphs.text: That blow was the last of the battle. Once out upon the road, Black Dog, in spite of his wound, showed a wonderful clean pair of heels and disappeared over the edge of the hill in half a minute. The captain, for his part, stood staring at the signboard like a bewildered man. Then he passed his hand over his eyes several times and at last turned back into the house.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 那一击是战斗的最后一击。一上路，黑狗尽管受了伤，却露出了一双干净的高跟鞋，半分钟后就消失在山边了。船长则站在那儿，像个困惑的人一样盯着招牌。然后他用手捂住眼睛好几次，最后才转身进屋。

paragraphs.text: I ran to fetch it, but I was quite unsteadied by all that had fallen out, and I broke one glass and fouled the tap, and while I was still getting in my own way, I heard a loud fall in the parlour, and running in, beheld the captain lying full length upon the floor. At the same instant my mother, alarmed by the cries and fighting, came running downstairs to help me. Between us we raised his head. He was breathing very loud and hard, but his eyes were closed and his face a horrible colour.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我跑去拿它，但我因为掉下来的东西而站不稳，我打碎了一个玻璃杯，弄脏了水龙头，当我还在妨碍自己的时候，我听到客厅里摔倒的一声巨响，我跑进去，看到船长全身躺在地板上。与此同时，我的母亲被哭声和打斗声吓坏了，跑下楼来帮助我。我们在我们之间抬起了头。他的呼吸声很大很急促，但他的眼睛紧闭，脸色惨白。

paragraphs.text: In the meantime, we had no idea what to do to help the captain, nor any other thought but that he had got his death-hurt in the scuffle with the stranger. I got the rum, to be sure, and tried to put it down his throat, but his teeth were tightly shut and his jaws as strong as iron. It was a happy relief for us when the door opened and Doctor Livesey came in, on his visit to my father.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 与此同时，我们不知道该怎么帮助船长，除了他在与陌生人的扭打中受重伤而死之外，我们也没有任何其他想法。当然，我拿到了朗姆酒，试图把它放进他的喉咙里，但他的牙齿紧闭，下巴坚如铁。当门打开，利夫西医生进来探望我父亲时，我们感到很高兴。

paragraphs.text: "Wounded? A fiddle-stick's end!" said the doctor. "No more wounded than you or I. The man has had a stroke, as I warned him. Now, Mrs. Hawkins, just you run upstairs to your husband and tell him, if possible, nothing about it. For my part, I must do my best to save this fellow's trebly worthless life; Jim, you get me a basin."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “受伤了？小提琴棒的尽头！”医生说。“受伤的人数并不比你我更多。正如我警告过他的那样，这个人中风了。现在，霍金斯太太，你跑上楼去找你丈夫，如果可能的话，什么也不告诉他。就我而言，我必须尽力挽救这个家伙毫无价值的生命；吉姆，你给我一个脸盆。”

paragraphs.text: When I got back with the basin, the doctor had already ripped up the captain's sleeve and exposed his great sinewy arm. It was tattooed in several places. "Here's luck," "A fair wind," and "Billy Bones his fancy," were very neatly and clearly executed on the forearm; and up near the shoulder there was a sketch of a gallows and a man hanging from it--done, as I thought, with great spirit.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 当我拿着脸盆回来时，医生已经撕开了船长的袖子，露出了他粗壮的大臂。它的几个地方都有纹身。“祝你好运”、“一阵好风”和“比利·骨头，他的幻想”，非常整齐、清晰地写在前臂上；在靠近肩膀的地方，有一个绞刑架的草图，上面挂着一个人——我认为，这是用伟大的精神完成的。

entries.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes: Sherlock Passage A

paragraphs.text: "Quite so," he answered, lighting a cigarette, and throwing himself down into an armchair. "You see, but you do not observe. The distinction is clear. For example, you have frequently seen the steps which lead up from the hall to this room."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “确实如此，”他回答道，点燃一支香烟，然后坐到扶手椅上。“你看，但你没有观察。区别很明显。例如，你经常看到从大厅通向这个房间的台阶。”

paragraphs.text: "Quite so! You have not observed. And yet you have seen. That is just my point. Now, I know that there are seventeen steps, because I have both seen and observed. By the way, since you are interested in these little problems, and since you are good enough to chronicle one or two of my trifling experiences, you may be interested in this." He threw over a sheet of thick, pink-tinted notepaper which had been lying open upon the table. "It came by the last post," said he. "Read it aloud."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “正是如此！你没有观察到。然而你已经看到了。这就是我的观点。现在，我知道有十七个步骤，因为我既看到又观察。顺便说一句，既然你对这些小问题感兴趣，又能够记录我的一两件琐碎经历，那么你可能会对这个感兴趣。”他扔了一张厚厚的粉红色信纸，这张信纸一直放在桌子上。“这是最后一个帖子来的，”他说。“大声读出来。”

entries.title: Pride and Prejudice: Pride and Prejudice Passage A

paragraphs.text: being gone to London only to get a large party for the ball; and a report soon followed that Mr. Bingley was to bring twelve ladies and seven gentlemen with him to the assembly. The girls grieved over such a number of ladies; but were comforted the day before the ball by hearing that, instead of twelve, he had brought only six with him from London, his five sisters and a cousin. And when the party entered the assembly-room, it consisted of only five altogether: Mr. Bingley, his two sisters, the husband of the eldest, and another young man.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 去伦敦只是为了举办一场大型舞会；很快就有报道称宾利先生将带着十二位女士和七位先生参加集会。女孩们为这么多的女士感到悲伤。但在舞会前一天，当得知他从伦敦只带来了六个人而不是十二个，即他的五个姐妹和一个表弟时，我们感到很欣慰。当一行人进入会议室时，总共只有五个人：彬格莱先生、他的两个姐妹、长子的丈夫和另一个年轻人。

entries.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes: Sherlock Passage B

paragraphs.text: "Not a bit, Doctor. Stay where you are. I am lost without my Boswell. And this promises to be interesting. It would be a pity to miss it."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “一点也不，博士。呆在原地。没有我的博斯韦尔，我就迷失了。这肯定会很有趣。错过了就可惜了。”

paragraphs.text: A slow and heavy step, which had been heard upon the stairs and in the passage, paused immediately outside the door. Then there was a loud and authoritative tap.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 楼梯上和过道上传来的缓慢而沉重的脚步声立刻在门外停了下来。然后是响亮而权威的敲击声。

paragraphs.text: "You had my note?" he asked with a deep harsh voice and a strongly marked German accent. "I told you that I would call." He looked from one to the other of us, as if uncertain which to address.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你有我的字条吗？”他用低沉刺耳的声音问道，带着浓重的德国口音。“我说过我会打电话的。”他看看我们中的一个，又看看另一个，似乎不确定该跟谁说话。

paragraphs.text: "Pray take a seat," said Holmes. "This is my friend and colleague, Dr. Watson, who is occasionally good enough to help me in my cases. Whom have I the honour to address?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “请坐，”福尔摩斯说。“这是我的朋友和同事，沃森医生，他偶尔会在我的案件中提供足够的帮助。我有幸向谁讲话？”

paragraphs.text: "You may address me as the Count Von Kramm, a Bohemian nobleman. I understand that this gentleman, your friend, is a man of honour and discretion, whom I may trust with a matter of the most extreme importance. If not, I should much prefer to communicate with you alone."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你可以称我为冯·克拉姆伯爵，一位波西米亚贵族。我知道这位先生，你的朋友，是一个有荣誉感和谨慎的人，我可以信任他来处理最重要的事情。如果没有的话，我更愿意单独和你交流。”

paragraphs.text: I rose to go, but Holmes caught me by the wrist and pushed me back into my chair. "It is both, or none," said he. "You may say before this gentleman anything which you may say to me."
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我起身要走，但福尔摩斯抓住了我的手腕，把我推回到椅子上。“要么两者兼而有之，要么都没有，”他说。“你对我说的话都可以在这位先生面前说。”

entries.title: Pride and Prejudice: Pride and Prejudice Passage B

paragraphs.text: Mr. Bingley inherited property to the amount of nearly a hundred thousand pounds from his father, who had intended to purchase an estate, but did not live to do it. Mr. Bingley intended it likewise, and sometimes made choice of his county; but, as he was now provided with a good house and the liberty of a manor, it was doubtful to many of those who best knew the easiness of his temper, whether he might not spend the remainder of his days at Netherfield, and leave the next generation to purchase.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 宾利先生从他父亲那里继承了近十万英镑的财产，他父亲原本打算购买一处庄园，但未能活着。彬格莱先生也有同样的意图，有时他会选择他的县。但是，由于他现在拥有一栋好房子和庄园的自由，许多最了解他脾气暴躁的人都怀疑他是否会在尼日斐花园度过余生，并留给下一代购买。

paragraphs.text: His sisters were very anxious for his having an estate of his own; but though he was now established only as a tenant, Miss Bingley was by no means unwilling to preside at his table; nor was Mrs. Hurst, who had married a man of more fashion than fortune, less disposed to consider his house as her home when it suited her. Mr. Bingley had not been of age two years when he was tempted, by an accidental recommendation, to look at Netherfield House. He did look at it, and into it, for half an hour; was pleased with the situation and the principal rooms, satisfied with what the owner said in its praise, and took it immediately.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 他的姐妹们非常渴望他拥有自己的庄园。尽管他现在只是作为一个佃户，彬格莱小姐却一点也不愿意主持他的餐桌。赫斯特夫人嫁给了一个比财富更时尚的男人，当她觉得合适的时候，她也毫不犹豫地把他的房子当作她的家。宾利先生在一次偶然的推荐下，想去参观尼日斐宫时，还不满两岁。他确实看了半个小时。对情况和正房很满意，对业主夸奖的话很满意，立即入住。

entries.title: The Time Machine: Time Machine Passage A

paragraphs.text: There was a minute's pause perhaps. The Psychologist seemed about to speak to me, but changed his mind. Then the Time Traveller put forth his finger towards the lever. "No," he said suddenly. "Lend me your hand." And turning to the Psychologist, he took that individual's hand in his own and told him to put out his forefinger. So that it was the Psychologist himself who sent forth the model Time Machine on its interminable voyage. We all saw the lever turn. I am absolutely certain there was no trickery. There was a breath of wind, and the lamp flame jumped. One of the candles on the mantel was blown out, and the little machine suddenly swung round, became indistinct, was seen as a ghost for a second perhaps, as an eddy of faintly glittering brass and ivory; and it was gone—vanished! Save for the lamp the table was bare.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 也许停顿了一分钟。心理学家似乎想跟我说话，但后来改变了主意。然后时间旅行者将手指伸向控制杆。“不，”他突然说道。“把手借给我。”然后转向心理学家，他握住那个人的手，让他伸出食指。因此，正是心理学家本人将时间机器模型送上了无尽的航程。我们都看到杠杆转动了。我绝对确定没有任何诡计。一阵风吹过，灯火跳动。壁炉架上的一根蜡烛被吹灭了，那台小机器突然旋转起来，变得模糊不清，一瞬间被视为幽灵，就像一个微弱闪光的黄铜和象牙的漩涡；然后它就消失了——消失了！除了台灯，桌子上空无一物。

paragraphs.text: The Psychologist recovered from his stupor, and suddenly looked under the table. At that the Time Traveller laughed cheerfully. "Well?" he said, with a reminiscence of the Psychologist. Then, getting up, he went to the tobacco jar on the mantel, and with his back to us began to fill his pipe.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 心理学家从恍惚中回过神来，突然看向桌子底下。时间旅行者闻言哈哈大笑。“出色地？”他回忆起心理学家说道。然后，他站起来，走向壁炉架上的烟草罐，背对着我们开始给他的烟斗装满烟斗。

paragraphs.text: "Certainly," said the Time Traveller, stooping to light a spill at the fire. Then he turned, lighting his pipe, to look at the Psychologist's face. (The Psychologist, to show that he was not unhinged, helped himself to a cigar and tried to light it uncut.) "What is more, I have a big machine nearly finished in there"—he indicated the laboratory—"and when that is put together I mean to have a journey on my own account."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “当然，”时间旅行者说着，弯下腰去点燃火堆。然后他转过身来，点着烟斗，看着心理学家的脸。（为了表明他没有精神错乱，心理学家给自己拿了一支雪茄，并试图将其点燃。）“更重要的是，我那里有一台即将完工的大机器”——他指着实验室——“当它组装起来时，我打算独自进行一次旅行。”

paragraphs.text: "Would you like to see the Time Machine itself?" asked the Time Traveller. And therewith, taking the lamp in his hand, he led the way down the long, draughty corridor to his laboratory. I remember vividly the flickering light, his queer, broad head in silhouette, the dance of the shadows, how we all followed him, puzzled but incredulous, and how there in the laboratory we beheld a larger edition of the little mechanism which we had seen vanish from before our eyes. Parts were of nickel, parts of ivory, parts had certainly been filed or sawn out of rock crystal. The thing was generally complete, but the twisted crystalline bars lay unfinished upon the bench beside some sheets of drawings, and I took one up for a better look at it. Quartz it seemed to be.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你想看看时间机器吗？”时间旅行者问道。说完，他手里拿着灯，沿着通风良好的长走廊，来到了他的实验室。我清楚地记得闪烁的灯光、他那奇怪、宽阔的头部轮廓、舞动的阴影、我们如何跟随他，困惑但难以置信，以及在实验室里我们如何看到一个放大版的小装置，而它却从我们眼前消失了。部分是镍的，部分是象牙的，部分肯定是用水晶锉或锯出来的。东西基本上已经完成了，但是扭曲的水晶棒还没有完成，放在长凳上，旁边还有几张图纸，我拿起一张以便更好地观察它。看起来是石英的。

paragraphs.text: The next Thursday I went again to Richmond—I suppose I was one of the Time Traveller's most constant guests—and, arriving late, found four or five men already assembled in his drawing-room. The Medical Man was standing before the fire with a sheet of paper in one hand and his watch in the other. I looked round for the Time Traveller, and—"It's half-past seven now," said the Medical Man. "I suppose we'd better have dinner?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: 下周四，我再次前往里士满——我想我是时间旅行者最常来的客人之一——到达较晚，发现四五个人已经聚集在他的客厅里。医生站在火边，一手拿着一张纸，另一只手拿着手表。我环顾四周寻找时间旅行者，然后——“现在已经七点半了，”医生说。“我想我们最好吃晚饭吧？”

paragraphs.text: "You've just come? It's rather odd. He's unavoidably detained. He asks me in this note to lead off with dinner at seven if he's not back. Says he'll explain when he comes."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你才来啊？这很奇怪。他不可避免地被拘留了。他在这张便条中要求我如果他没有回来的话，就在七点开始吃晚饭。说他来的时候会解释的。”

entries.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn: Huckleberry Finn Passage A

paragraphs.text: "Yes, he's got a father, but you can't never find him these days. He used to lay drunk with the hogs in the tanyard, but he hain't been seen in these parts for a year or more."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “是的，他有父亲，但现在你永远找不到他了。他过去常常和制革厂里的猪一起喝醉，但已经有一年多没有在这些地方见过他了。”

paragraphs.text: They talked it over, and they was going to rule me out, because they said every boy must have a family or somebody to kill, or else it wouldn't be fair and square for the others. Well, nobody could think of anything to do—everybody was stumped, and set still. I was most ready to cry; but all at once I thought of a way, and so I offered them Miss Watson—they could kill her.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 他们商量了一下，打算把我排除在外，因为他们说每个男孩都必须有一个家庭或一个可以杀死的人，否则对其他人来说就不公平。好吧，没有人想出什么办法——每个人都被难住了，一动不动。我快要哭了。但我突然想到了一个办法，于是我向他们提供了沃森小姐——他们可以杀了她。

paragraphs.text: "Stuff! stealing cattle and such things ain't robbery; it's burglary," says Tom Sawyer. "We ain't burglars. That ain't no sort of style. We are highwaymen. We stop stages and carriages on the road, with masks on, and kill the people and take their watches and money."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “东西！偷牛等行为不是抢劫；这是入室盗窃，”汤姆·索亚说。“我们不是窃贼。这可不是什么风格。我们是强盗。我们戴着面具，拦住路上的舞台和马车，杀死人们，拿走他们的手表和钱。”

paragraphs.text: "Oh, certainly. It's best. Some authorities think different, but mostly it's considered best to kill them—except some that you bring to the cave here, and keep them till they're ransomed."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “哦，当然。最好了。有些当局有不同的看法，但大多数人认为最好杀死他们——除了一些你带到这里的山洞里的人，并保留他们直到他们被赎回。”

paragraphs.text: "Well, Ben Rogers, if I was as ignorant as you I wouldn't let on. Kill the women? No; nobody ever saw anything in the books like that. You fetch them to the cave, and you're always as polite as pie to them; and by-and-by they fall in love with you, and never want to go home any more."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “好吧，本·罗杰斯，如果我像你一样无知，我就不会表露出来。杀女人？不;没有人在书中看到过这样的内容。你把他们带到山洞里，对他们总是彬彬有礼。渐渐地，他们就爱上了你，再也不想回家了。”

paragraphs.text: "Well, if that's the way I'm agreed, but I don't take no stock in it. Mighty soon we'll have the cave so cluttered up with women, and fellows waiting to be ransomed, that there won't be no place for the robbers. But go ahead, I ain't got nothing to say."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “好吧，如果我同意的话，但我不会对此置之不理。很快我们的山洞就会挤满女人和等待赎回的人，强盗将无处容身。不过你说吧，我没什么可说的。”

paragraphs.text: So they all made fun of him, and called him cry-baby, and that made him mad, and he said he would go straight and tell all the secrets. But Tom give him five cents to keep quiet, and said we would all go home and meet next week, and rob somebody and kill some people.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 所以他们都取笑他，叫他爱哭鬼，这让他很生气，他说他会直接告诉他所有的秘密。但汤姆给了他五美分让他保持安静，并说我们下周都会回家见面，抢劫一些人，杀死一些人。

paragraphs.text: Ben Rogers said he couldn't get out much, only Sundays, and so he wanted to begin next Sunday; but all the boys said it would be wicked to do it on Sunday, and that settled the thing. They agreed to get together and fix a day as soon as they could, and then we elected Tom Sawyer first captain and Jo Harper second captain of the Gang, and so started home.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 本·罗杰斯说他不能出去太多，只能在周日出去，所以他想从下周日开始。但所有的男孩都说周日这样做是邪恶的，事情就这么解决了。他们同意尽快聚在一起并确定一天，然后我们选举汤姆·索亚为帮派第一队长，乔·哈珀为帮派第二队长，然后就开始回家了。

paragraphs.text: I clumb up the shed and crept into my window just before day was breaking. My new clothes was all greased up and clayey, and I was dog-tired.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 天亮之前，我爬上棚屋，爬进窗户。我的新衣服沾满了油污，沾满泥土，我累坏了。

paragraphs.text: Well, I got a good going-over in the morning from old Miss Watson on account of my clothes; but the widow she didn't scold, but only cleaned off the grease and clay, and looked so sorry that I thought I would behave a while if I could. Then Miss Watson she took me in the closet and prayed, but nothing come of it. She told me to pray every day, and whatever I asked for I would get it. But it warn't so. I tried it. Once I got a fish-line, but no hooks. It warn't any good to me without hooks. I tried for the hooks three or four times, but somehow I couldn't make it work. By-and-by, one day, I asked Miss Watson to try for me, but she said I was a fool. She never told me why, and I couldn't make it out no way.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 嗯，早上老沃森小姐因为我的衣服问题对我进行了仔细的检查。但寡妇她并没有责骂，只是把油污和泥土清理干净，一脸抱歉的样子，我想如果可以的话我还是乖一点吧。然后沃森小姐把我带到衣柜里祈祷，但没有任何结果。她告诉我每天祈祷，无论我祈求什么，我都会得到。但事实并非如此。我试过了。有一次我得到了一条鱼线，但没有鱼钩。没有钩子对我来说没有任何好处。我尝试了三四次钩子，但不知何故我无法让它发挥作用。有一天，我请沃森小姐为我试一试，但她说我是个傻瓜。她从来没有告诉过我原因，我也无法理解。

entries.title: The Federalist Papers: Federalist Passage A

paragraphs.text: Among the many objects to which a wise and free people find it necessary to direct their attention, that of providing for their SAFETY seems to be the first. The SAFETY of the people doubtless has relation to a great variety of circumstances and considerations, and consequently affords great latitude to those who wish to define it precisely and comprehensively.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在明智和自由的人们发现有必要关注的许多目标中，保障他们的安全似乎是首要的。人民的安全无疑与各种各样的情况和考虑因素有关，因此为那些希望准确而全面地定义它的人提供了很大的自由度。

paragraphs.text: The JUST causes of war, for the most part, arise either from violation of treaties or from direct violence. America has already formed treaties with no less than six foreign nations, and all of them, except Prussia, are maritime, and therefore able to annoy and injure us. She has also extensive commerce with Portugal, Spain, and Britain, and, with respect to the two latter, has, in addition, the circumstance of neighborhood to attend to.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 战争的正义起因在很大程度上要么源于违反条约，要么源于直接暴力。美国已经与不少于六个国家签订了条约，而除普鲁士外，所有这些国家都是海上国家，因此能够骚扰和伤害我们。她还与葡萄牙、西班牙和英国有广泛的贸易往来，对于后两者，她还需要注意邻里的情况。

entries.title: Meditations: Meditations Passage A

paragraphs.text: XXII. Either this world is a κόσμος or comely piece, because all disposed and governed by certain order: or if it be a mixture, though confused, yet still it is a comely piece. For is it possible that in thee there should be any beauty at all, and that in the whole world there should be nothing but disorder and confusion? and all things in it too, by natural different properties one from another differenced and distinguished; and yet all through diffused, and by natural sympathy, one to another united, as they are?
paragraphs.translation_zh: 第二十二．要么这个世界是一个 κόσμος 或美丽的部分，因为一切都由一定的秩序安排和支配；或者如果它是一个混合物，尽管混乱，但它仍然是一个美丽的部分。因为有可能在你身上有任何美丽，而在整个世界上除了无序和混乱之外什么都没有吗？其中的所有事物也因自然不同的属性而彼此区别和区别。然而，所有这些都是通过分散的、自然的同情心，使彼此之间团结起来，就像他们现在这样吗？

entries.title: The Federalist Papers: Federalist Passage B

paragraphs.text: Whenever, and from whatever causes, it might happen, and happen it would, that any one of these nations or confederacies should rise on the scale of political importance much above the degree of her neighbors, that moment would those neighbors behold her with envy and with fear. Both those passions would lead them to countenance, if not to promote, whatever might promise to diminish her importance; and would also restrain them from measures calculated to advance or even to secure her prosperity. Much time would not be necessary to enable her to discern these unfriendly dispositions. She would soon begin, not only to lose confidence in her neighbors, but also to feel a disposition equally unfavorable to them. Distrust naturally creates distrust, and by nothing is good-will and kind conduct more speedily changed than by invidious jealousies and uncandid imputations, whether expressed or implied.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 无论何时，无论出于何种原因，这些国家或联盟中的任何一个国家或联盟的政治重要性将远远高于其邻国，那一刻，那些邻国就会以嫉妒和恐惧的目光注视着她。这两种热情都会导致他们支持（如果不是提倡的话）任何可能削弱她重要性的事情。并且还会限制他们采取旨在促进甚至确保她的繁荣的措施。她不需要太多时间就能辨别出这些不友好的性格。她很快就会开始不仅对邻居失去信心，而且也会感到一种对他们同样不利的性格。不信任自然会产生不信任，没有什么比恶意的嫉妒和不诚实的指责（无论是明示的还是暗示的）更能更快地改变善意和善良的行为。

paragraphs.text: The North is generally the region of strength, and many local circumstances render it probable that the most Northern of the proposed confederacies would, at a period not very distant, be unquestionably more formidable than any of the others. No sooner would this become evident than the NORTHERN HIVE would excite the same ideas and sensations in the more southern parts of America which it formerly did in the southern parts of Europe. Nor does it appear to be a rash conjecture that its young swarms might often be tempted to gather honey in the more blooming fields and milder air of their luxurious and more delicate neighbors.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 北方通常是实力较强的地区，许多当地情况使得拟议中的同盟中最北方的一个在不久的将来无疑会比其他任何一个同盟都更强大。一旦这一点变得明显，北方蜂巢就会在美国南部地区激起与以前在欧洲南部地区相同的想法和感觉。这似乎也不是一个轻率的猜想，它的幼蜂群可能经常受到诱惑，到它们奢华而精致的邻居的更盛开的田野和更温和的空气中采集蜂蜜。

entries.title: Meditations: Meditations Passage B

paragraphs.text: XXXVII. Let that of Heraclitus never be out of thy mind, that the death of earth, is water, and the death of water, is air; and the death of air, is fire; and so on the contrary. Remember him also who was ignorant whither the way did lead, and how that reason being the thing by which all things in the world are administered, and which men are continually and most inwardly conversant with: yet is the thing, which ordinarily they are most in opposition with, and how those things which daily happen among them, cease not daily to be strange unto them, and that we should not either speak, or do anything as men in their sleep, by opinion and bare imagination: for then we think we speak and do, and that we must not be as children, who follow their father's example; for best reason alleging their bare καθότι παρειλήφαμεν; or, as by successive tradition from our forefathers we have received it.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 三十七。永远不要忘记赫拉克利特的观点：大地的死亡是水，水的死亡是空气；空气的死亡就是火；反之亦然。还要记住他，他不知道这条路通向何方，以及理性是如何管理世界上所有事物的，人们不断地、最内心地熟悉理性：然而，理性是他们通常最反对的东西，以及每天发生在他们中间的事情如何对他们来说每天都不是陌生的，我们不应该像人在睡梦中那样，通过意见和纯粹的想象来说话或做任何事情：因为那时我们认为我们在说话和做事，而我们不能像孩子一样，跟随父亲的脚步；出于最好的理由声称他们裸露 καθότι παρειλήφαμεν；或者，按照我们祖先的连续传统，我们已经接受了它。

paragraphs.text: XXXVIII. Even as if any of the gods should tell thee, Thou shalt certainly die to-morrow, or next day, thou wouldst not, except thou wert extremely base and pusillanimous, take it for a great benefit, rather to die the next day after, than to-morrow; (for alas, what is the difference!) so, for the same reason, think it no great matter to die rather many years after, than the very next day.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 三十八。即使任何一位神明告诉你，你明天或后天肯定会死，除非你极其卑鄙和胆怯，否则你不会认为这是一个巨大的好处，宁愿后天死，也不愿明天死；除非你极其卑鄙和胆怯，否则你不会认为这是一个巨大的好处。 （唉，有什么区别！）因此，出于同样的原因，认为多年之后死去比第二天死去也没什么大不了的。

entries.title: On the Origin of Species: Origin of Species Passage A

paragraphs.text: Close investigation, in most cases, will bring naturalists to an agreement how to rank doubtful forms. Yet it must be confessed, that it is in the best-known countries that we find the greatest number of forms of doubtful value. I have been struck with the fact, that if any animal or plant in a state of nature be highly useful to man, or from any cause closely attract his attention, varieties of it will almost universally be found recorded. These varieties, moreover, will be often ranked by some authors as species. Look at the common oak, how closely it has been studied; yet a German author makes more than a dozen species out of forms, which are very generally considered as varieties; and in this country the highest botanical authorities and practical men can be quoted to show that the sessile and pedunculated oaks are either good and distinct species or mere varieties.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在大多数情况下，仔细的调查将使博物学家就如何对可疑形式进行排序达成一致。但必须承认，正是在最著名的国家，我们发现了最多数量的可疑价值形式。令我震惊的事实是，如果任何自然状态下的动物或植物对人类非常有用，或者出于某种原因密切吸引人类的注意，那么它的各种变体几乎都会被记录下来。此外，一些作者经常将这些变种列为物种。看看普通橡树，它被研究得多么仔细；然而，一位德国作家却从形式中创造了十几个物种，这些形式通常被认为是变体；在这个国家，可以引用最高植物学权威和实干家的话说，无梗橡树和有蒂橡树要么是优良的独特物种，要么只是品种。

paragraphs.text: Certainly no clear line of demarcation has as yet been drawn between species and sub-species—that is, the forms which in the opinion of some naturalists come very near to, but do not quite arrive at the rank of species; or, again, between sub-species and well-marked varieties, or between lesser varieties and individual differences. These differences blend into each other in an insensible series; and a series impresses the mind with the idea of an actual passage.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 当然，物种和亚种之间还没有明确的界限——也就是说，在一些博物学家看来，这些形式非常接近物种的等级，但还没有完全达到物种的等级；或者，亚种和明显品种之间，或较小品种和个体差异之间。这些差异在一个不明显的系列中相互融合；一系列作品以真实段落的想法给人们留下深刻的印象。

paragraphs.text: It need not be supposed that all varieties or incipient species necessarily attain the rank of species. They may whilst in this incipient state become extinct, or they may endure as varieties for very long periods, as has been shown to be the case by Mr. Wollaston with the varieties of certain fossil land-shells in Madeira. If a variety were to flourish so as to exceed in numbers the parent species, it would then rank as the species, and the species as the variety; or it might come to supplant and exterminate the parent species; or both might co-exist, and both rank as independent species. But we shall hereafter have to return to this subject.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 不必假设所有变种或初期物种都必然达到物种的等级。它们可能在这个初期状态就灭绝了，或者它们可能作为变种持续很长一段时间，正如沃拉斯顿先生对马德拉群岛某些化石陆地贝壳变种的情况所表明的那样。如果一个变种繁衍生息，数量超过其亲本种，那么它就被列为物种，而该物种则被列为变种；或者它可能会取代并消灭亲本物种；或者两者可能共存，并且都被列为独立物种。但我们稍后将不得不回到这个话题。

paragraphs.text: From these remarks it will be seen that I look at the term species, as one arbitrarily given for the sake of convenience to a set of individuals closely resembling each other, and that it does not essentially differ from the term variety, which is given to less distinct and more fluctuating forms. The term variety, again, in comparison with mere individual differences, is also applied arbitrarily, and for mere convenience sake.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 从这些评论中可以看出，我将“物种”一词视为为了方便起见而任意赋予一组彼此非常相似的个体的术语，并且它与“多样性”一词没有本质上的区别，后者被赋予不太明显和更波动的形式。同样，与单纯的个体差异相比，“多样性”一词也是任意使用的，并且仅仅是为了方便起见。

entries.title: The Federalist Papers: Federalist Passage C

paragraphs.text: Carthage, though a commercial republic, was the aggressor in the very war that ended in her destruction. Hannibal had carried her arms into the heart of Italy and to the gates of Rome, before Scipio, in turn, gave him an overthrow in the territories of Carthage, and made a conquest of the commonwealth.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 迦太基虽然是一个商业共和国，但却是那场最终导致其毁灭的战争的侵略者。汉尼拔带着她的武器进入了意大利的心脏地带，直抵罗马的大门，然后西庇阿反过来在迦太基领土上推翻了他，并征服了联邦。

paragraphs.text: Venice, in later times, figured more than once in wars of ambition, till, becoming an object to the other Italian states, Pope Julius II. found means to accomplish that formidable league,(9) which gave a deadly blow to the power and pride of this haughty republic.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在后来的时代，威尼斯不止一次地出现在野心战争中，直到成为教皇朱利叶斯二世（Pope Julius II）其他意大利国家的目标。找到了实现这一强大联盟的方法，（9）这对这个傲慢的共和国的权力和骄傲造成了致命的打击。

paragraphs.text: The provinces of Holland, till they were overwhelmed in debts and taxes, took a leading and conspicuous part in the wars of Europe. They had furious contests with England for the dominion of the sea, and were among the most persevering and most implacable of the opponents of Louis XIV.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 荷兰各省在债务和税收不堪重负之前，一直在欧洲战争中发挥着主导作用。他们与英国争夺制海权，展开了激烈的争夺，是路易十四最顽强、最顽固的反对者之一。

paragraphs.text: In the government of Britain the representatives of the people compose one branch of the national legislature. Commerce has been for ages the predominant pursuit of that country. Few nations, nevertheless, have been more frequently engaged in war; and the wars in which that kingdom has been engaged have, in numerous instances, proceeded from the people.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在英国政府中，人民代表组成国家立法机关的一个分支。多年来，商业一直是该国的主要追求。然而，很少有国家比它更频繁地卷入战争。在许多情况下，该王国所参与的战争都是由人民发起的。

paragraphs.text: There have been, if I may so express it, almost as many popular as royal wars. The cries of the nation and the importunities of their representatives have, upon various occasions, dragged their monarchs into war, or continued them in it, contrary to their inclinations, and sometimes contrary to the real interests of the State. In that memorable struggle for superiority between the rival houses of AUSTRIA and BOURBON, which so long kept Europe in a flame, it is well known that the antipathies of the English against the French, seconding the ambition, or rather the avarice, of a favorite leader,(10) protracted the war beyond the limits marked out by sound policy, and for a considerable time in opposition to the views of the court.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 如果我可以这样表达的话，几乎和皇家战争一样受欢迎。民族的呼声和代表们的强求，在不同的场合将他们的君主拖入战争，或让他们继续卷入战争，这违背了他们的意愿，有时甚至违背了国家的真正利益。在奥地利和波旁王朝之间令人难忘的争夺优势的斗争中，这场斗争长期以来让欧洲陷入了困境，众所周知，英国人对法国人的反感，支持了一位最喜欢的领导人的野心，或者更确切地说是贪婪，（10）使战争超出了合理政策所规定的范围，并且在相当长的时间内与宫廷的观点相悖。

paragraphs.text: From this summary of what has taken place in other countries, whose situations have borne the nearest resemblance to our own, what reason can we have to confide in those reveries which would seduce us into an expectation of peace and cordiality between the members of the present confederacy, in a state of separation? Have we not already seen enough of the fallacy and extravagance of those idle theories which have amused us with promises of an exemption from the imperfections, weaknesses and evils incident to society in every shape? Is it not time to awake from the deceitful dream of a golden age, and to adopt as a practical maxim for the direction of our political conduct that we, as well as the other inhabitants of the globe, are yet remote from the happy empire of perfect wisdom and perfect virtue?
paragraphs.translation_zh: 从对其他国家所发生的事情的总结来看，这些国家的情况与我们国家的情况最相似，我们有什么理由相信那些会诱使我们期待目前处于分离状态的邦联成员之间和平与友好的幻想呢？我们还没有充分认识到那些空洞的理论的谬误和夸张吗？这些理论曾许诺我们可以免受各种形式的社会所带来的不完美、弱点和邪恶的影响，从而使我们感到可笑？难道现在还不是从黄金时代的欺骗性梦想中醒来，并把我们以及地球上的其他居民都远离完美智慧和完美美德的幸福帝国作为我们政治行为方向的实际格言的时候吗？

paragraphs.text: IT IS sometimes asked, with an air of seeming triumph, what inducements could the States have, if disunited, to make war upon each other? It would be a full answer to this question to say--precisely the same inducements which have, at different times, deluged in blood all the nations in the world. But, unfortunately for us, the question admits of a more particular answer. There are causes of differences within our immediate contemplation, of the tendency of which, even under the restraints of a federal constitution, we have had sufficient experience to enable us to form a judgment of what might be expected if those restraints were removed.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 有时有人带着看似胜利的神情问，如果各州不团结，有什么诱因相互发动战争？这将是对这个问题的完整回答——正是同样的诱因，在不同的时期，使世界上所有国家都陷入了鲜血之中。但是，对我们来说不幸的是，这个问题有一个更具体的答案。在我们直接考虑的范围内存在着一些差异的原因，即使在联邦宪法的限制下，我们也有足够的经验使我们能够对这些限制被消除后可能出现的情况做出判断。

entries.title: Scientific American Supplement, No. 508: Scientific American Passage A

paragraphs.text: On the 20th of December, 1884, my excavating was finished. To-day the Oubliettes of Gargas are obstructed with the clay that it was impossible to carry elsewhere. The animals that I thus collected in the well were the following: The great bear (in abundance), the little bear (a variety of the preceding), the hyena, and the wolf. The pockets contained nearly entire skeletons of these species. How had the animals been able to penetrate this well? It is difficult to admit that it was through the aperture that I have mentioned. I endeavored to ascertain whether there was not another communication with the Gargas grotto, and had the satisfaction of finding a fissure that ended in the cave, and that probably was wider at the epoch at which the place served as a lair for the bear and hyena.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 1884年12月20日，我的挖掘工作结束了。如今，加尔加斯的地牢被无法运往其他地方的粘土所堵塞。我在井里收集的动物如下：大熊（大量）、小熊（前面的变种）、鬣狗和狼。这些口袋里几乎装满了这些物种的整个骨骼。动物们是如何钻进这口井的呢？很难承认它是通过我提到的光圈进行的。我努力确定是否与加尔加斯石窟没有其他联系，并满意地发现了一条终止于洞穴的裂缝，并且在该地方作为熊和鬣狗的巢穴的时代，这条裂缝可能更宽。

entries.title: The Time Machine: Time Machine Passage B

paragraphs.text: "Presently I thought what a fool I was to get wet. I stood up and looked round me. A colossal figure, carved apparently in some white stone, loomed indistinctly beyond the rhododendrons through the hazy downpour. But all else of the world was invisible.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “现在我觉得自己被淋湿真是个傻瓜。我站起来环顾四周。一个巨大的人物，显然是用白色石头雕刻而成的，在朦胧的倾盆大雨中，在杜鹃花丛中若隐若现。但世界上的其他一切都是看不见的。

paragraphs.text: "I looked up again at the crouching white shape, and the full temerity of my voyage came suddenly upon me. What might appear when that hazy curtain was altogether withdrawn? What might not have happened to men? What if cruelty had grown into a common passion? What if in this interval the race had lost its manliness, and had developed into something inhuman, unsympathetic, and overwhelmingly powerful? I might seem some old-world savage animal, only the more dreadful and disgusting for our common likeness—a foul creature to be incontinently slain.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “我再次抬头看着那蹲伏的白色身影，我的航行突然充满了鲁莽。当那层朦胧的帷幕完全拉开时，会出现什么？有什么事情可能不会发生在男人身上呢？如果残忍已经发展成为一种共同的激情怎么办？如果在这段时间里，人类失去了男子气概，并发展成为一种不人道、缺乏同情心、极其强大的东西，那又会怎样呢？我可能看起来像某种旧世界的野蛮动物，只是比我们共同的相似之处更可怕和恶心——一种被不节制地杀死的肮脏生物。

entries.title: Scientific American Supplement, No. 508: Scientific American Passage B

paragraphs.text: The pitcher plant undoubtedly derives its principal nourishment from the insects it eats. It, too--unlike most other carnivorous plants, which, when the quantity of food with which they have to deal is in excess of their powers of digestion, succumb to the effort and die--appears to find it easy to devour any number of insects, small or large, the operation being with it simply a question of time. Flies, beetles, or even cockroaches, at the expiration of three or four days at most, disappear, nothing being left of them save their wings and other hard, parts of their bodies.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 毫无疑问，猪笼草的主要营养来自它所吃的昆虫。它也不像大多数其他食肉植物，当它们必须处理的食物量超过它们的消化能力时，就会屈服于努力并死亡——它似乎很容易吞食任何数量的昆虫，无论大小，行动只是时间问题。苍蝇、甲虫，甚至蟑螂，最多三四天后就会消失，除了翅膀和身体其他坚硬的部分外，什么也没有留下。

paragraphs.text: But our object now is less to point out how buds are to be developed into fully expanded flowers than to show how they were to be formed in plenty, and the plants preserved in robust health year after year. One of the simplest and surest modes of reaching this desirable end is to adopt a system of semi-tropical treatment for two months or so after flowering. The moment or even before the late blooms fade, the plants should be pruned if necessary. Few plants bear the knife better than camellias, though it is folly to cut them unless they are too tall or too large for their quarters or have grown out of form. As a rule healthy camellias produce sufficient or even a redundancy of shoots without cutting back; but should they need pruning, after flowering is the best time to perform the operation.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 但我们现在的目的不是指出花蕾如何发育成完全展开的花朵，而是展示它们如何大量形成，以及植物如何年复一年地保持健康。达到这一理想目标的最简单、最可靠的方式之一是在开花后采用两个月左右的亚热带处理系统。如有必要，应在晚花凋谢时或什至之前对植物进行修剪。很少有植物比山茶花更耐刀，不过除非山茶花太高或太大，或者已经长得不合适，否则砍它们是愚蠢的。一般来说，健康的山茶花会产生足够甚至多余的芽，而不会减少芽。但如果需要修剪，开花后是进行手术的最佳时间。

entries.title: On the Origin of Species: Origin of Species Passage B

paragraphs.text: I am well aware that this doctrine of natural selection, exemplified in the above imaginary instances, is open to the same objections which were at first urged against Sir Charles Lyell's noble views on "the modern changes of the earth, as illustrative of geology;" but we now very seldom hear the action, for instance, of the coast-waves, called a trifling and insignificant cause, when applied to the excavation of gigantic valleys or to the formation of the longest lines of inland cliffs. Natural selection can act only by the preservation and accumulation of infinitesimally small inherited modifications, each profitable to the preserved being; and as modern geology has almost banished such views as the excavation of a great valley by a single diluvial wave, so will natural selection, if it be a true principle, banish the belief of the continued creation of new organic beings, or of any great and sudden modification in their structure.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我很清楚，在上述想象的例子中所例证的自然选择学说，也可能会招致同样的反对意见，这些反对意见最初是针对查尔斯·莱尔爵士关于“地球的现代变化，作为地质学的例证”的崇高观点提出的。但是，我们现在很少听到海岸波浪的作用，当它应用于开挖巨大的山谷或形成最长的内陆悬崖时，海岸波浪被称为微不足道的和微不足道的原因。自然选择只能通过保存和积累无限小的遗传修饰来发挥作用，每一个修饰都对被保存的生物有利。正如现代地质学几乎消除了诸如一次洪流冲刷出一个大山谷之类的观点一样，自然选择如果是一个真实的原理，也将消除新的有机生物不断创造的信念，或者其结构发生任何巨大而突然的改变的信念。

paragraphs.text: It must have struck most naturalists as a strange anomaly that, in the case of both animals and plants, species of the same family and even of the same genus, though agreeing closely with each other in almost their whole organisation, yet are not rarely, some of them hermaphrodites, and some of them unisexual. But if, in fact, all hermaphrodites do occasionally intercross with other individuals, the difference between hermaphrodites and unisexual species, as far as function is concerned, becomes very small.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 大多数博物学家肯定认为这是一种奇怪的反常现象，就动物和植物而言，同一科甚至同一属的物种虽然在几乎整个组织上彼此非常一致，但并不罕见，其中有些是雌雄同体，有些是单性的。但事实上，如果所有雌雄同体偶尔与其他个体杂交，那么就功能而言，雌雄同体和单性物种之间的差异就变得非常小。

entries.title: Scientific American Supplement, No. 508: Scientific American Passage C

paragraphs.text: Yucatan is, and has been for ages past, quite free from earthquakes, while all surrounding countries are from time to time convulsed. This immunity may be due to the vast caverns and numerous great wells existing throughout the land. Pliny the Elder was of opinion that if numerous deep wells were made in the earth to serve as outlets for the gases that disturb its upper strata, the strength of the earthquakes would be diminished, and if we may judge by Yucatan, Pliny was right in his conjectures. After him, other scientists who have carefully studied the subject have expressed the same opinion with regard to the efficacy of large wells.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 尤卡坦半岛多年来一直没有发生地震，而周边国家却时不时发生地震。这种免疫力可能是由于这片土地上存在着巨大的洞穴和众多的大井。老普林尼认为，如果在地球上打许多深井，作为扰动上层地层的气体的出口，地震的强度就会减弱，如果我们可以根据尤卡坦半岛来判断，普林尼的猜想是正确的。在他之后，其他仔细研究该主题的科学家也对大井的功效表达了相同的观点。

paragraphs.text: It will be observed that this insect is composed of thirteen segments from head to tail, which is a distinctive characteristic of all insects both in the larval and perfect states; but in the case of this and most other caterpillars these segments are sharply defined and readily recognized. It will also be noticed that the three segments or "joints" nearest the head bear a pair of legs each; these are the real feet, or claspers, as they are sometimes termed, which develop into the feet of the future butterfly. There are four pairs of false feet or suckers, which adhere to the ground by suction, and which disappear in the butterfly. On the last or tail end is a fifth pair of suckers also, which can attach themselves to a surface with considerable force, as any one can attest who has noticed the wrigglings of one of these caterpillars when feeling for new feeding ground.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 可以观察到，这种昆虫从头到尾由十三节组成，这是所有昆虫在幼虫和完美状态下的显着特征；但对于这种毛毛虫和大多数其他毛毛虫来说，这些部分的界限很明确并且很容易识别。还应该注意到，最靠近头部的三个节段或“关节”各有一对腿；这些是真正的脚，或者有时被称为“脚掌”，它们会发育成未来蝴蝶的脚。有四对假脚或吸盘，通过吸力粘附在地面上，并消失在蝴蝶体内。最后或尾端还有第五对吸盘，它们可以用相当大的力将自己附着在表面上，任何人都可以证明谁注意到这些毛毛虫在寻找新的觅食地时会蠕动。

paragraphs.text: The wings derive their chief coloring from the covering of scales, which lie on like slates on a roof, and are attached in a similar manner. A small portion of the wing magnified is represented at the bottom right hand corner, and detached scales more highly magnified next to it, exhibiting somewhat the form of battledoors.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 翅膀的主要颜色来自于鳞片的覆盖，鳞片就像屋顶上的石板一样，并以类似的方式连接。机翼的一小部分被放大显示在右下角，旁边的分离鳞片被放大得更高，呈现出战门的形状。

paragraphs.text: Another well known insect is illustrated in the figure in the upper portion--the peacock butterfly (Vanessa Io). The curious spiked and spotted caterpillar feeds upon the common nettle. This beautiful butterfly--common in most districts--is brilliantly colored and figured on the upper side of the wings, but only of a mottled brown on the under surface, somewhat resembling a dried and brown leaf, so that it is no easy matter to detect the conspicuous, brightly-decked insect when it alights from flight upon foliage, and brings its wings together over its back after the manner of butterflies. At the left-hand corner is seen the head of the insect, magnified, showing the long spiral tongue.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 上图展示了另一种众所周知的昆虫——孔雀蝴蝶（Vanessa Io）。好奇的尖刺和斑点毛毛虫以荨麻为食。这种美丽的蝴蝶在大多数地区都很常见，颜色鲜艳，翅膀上侧有图案，但下表面只有棕色斑点，有点像干枯的棕色叶子，因此，当这种引人注目的、装饰鲜艳的昆虫从树叶上飞下来，并像蝴蝶一样将翅膀并拢在背上时，很难发现它。在左角可以看到放大的昆虫头部，显示出长长的螺旋舌头。

paragraphs.text: Mrs. Mary Treat, an American naturalist, made, a few years ago, several experiments upon the plants of this species to be found in Florida; and to the labors of this lady the writer has been indebted, in some measure, in the preparation of this paper.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 几年前，美国博物学家玛丽·特雷特夫人对佛罗里达州发现的这种植物进行了多次实验。在某种程度上，作者在准备本文的过程中要感谢这位女士的辛劳。

paragraphs.text: In instances in which experiments have been made with fresh, raw beef or mutton, the meat has been covered in a few hours with the secretions of the leaves, and the blood extracted from it. There is, however, one difference between the digesting powers of the leaves when exercised upon insects or upon meat. Even if the bodies of insects have become putrid, the plant, as has already been stated, has no difficulty in assimilating them; but as regards meat, it is only when it is perfectly sweet that the secretions of the leaves will act upon it.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在用新鲜的生牛肉或羊肉进行实验的情况下，肉在几个小时内就被叶子的分泌物和从中提取的血液覆盖。然而，当对昆虫或肉类进行叶子的消化能力时，它们之间存在一个差异。即使昆虫的尸体已经腐烂，正如已经说过的，植物也能毫无困难地吸收它们；但对于肉来说，只有在完全甜的时候，叶子的分泌物才会作用于它。

entries.title: On the Origin of Species: Origin of Species Passage C

paragraphs.text: Bears on natural selection. The term used in a wide sense. Geometrical powers of increase. Rapid increase of naturalised animals and plants. Nature of the checks to increase. Competition universal. Effects of climate. Protection from the number of individuals. Complex relations of all animals and plants throughout nature. Struggle for life most severe between individuals and varieties of the same species; often severe between species of the same genus. The relation of organism to organism the most important of all relations.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 取决于自然选择。该术语的使用范围很广。几何增长幂。归化动植物迅速增加。检查性质增加。竞争普遍。气候的影响。保护免受人数的影响。自然界中所有动物和植物之间的复杂关系。同一物种的个体和变体之间的生存斗争最为激烈；同一属的物种之间通常很严重。有机体与有机体的关系是所有关系中最重要的。

paragraphs.text: A struggle for existence inevitably follows from the high rate at which all organic beings tend to increase. Every being, which during its natural lifetime produces several eggs or seeds, must suffer destruction during some period of its life, and during some season or occasional year, otherwise, on the principle of geometrical increase, its numbers would quickly become so inordinately great that no country could support the product. Hence, as more individuals are produced than can possibly survive, there must in every case be a struggle for existence, either one individual with another of the same species, or with the individuals of distinct species, or with the physical conditions of life. It is the doctrine of Malthus applied with manifold force to the whole animal and vegetable kingdoms; for in this case there can be no artificial increase of food, and no prudential restraint from marriage. Although some species may be now increasing, more or less rapidly, in numbers, all cannot do so, for the world would not hold them.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 所有有机生物的高速增长不可避免地会导致生存斗争。每一个生物，在其自然寿命中产生数个卵或种子，都必须在其生命的某个时期、某个季节或偶尔的一年中遭受毁灭，否则，根据几何增长的原理，其数量将很快变得如此巨大，以至于没有一个国家能够支持这种产品。因此，当产生的个体数量超过了可能生存的数量时，在任何情况下都必然存在生存斗争，要么是一个个体与同一物种的另一个个体，要么是与不同物种的个体，或者是与生命的物理条件。这是马尔萨斯的学说，以多方面的力量应用于整个动物和植物界。因为在这种情况下，就不能人为地增加食物，也不能审慎地限制婚姻。尽管某些物种的数量现在可能或多或少地迅速增加，但所有物种都不能这样做，因为世界容不下它们。

paragraphs.text: In a state of nature almost every plant produces seed, and amongst animals there are very few which do not annually pair. Hence we may confidently assert, that all plants and animals are tending to increase at a geometrical ratio, that all would most rapidly stock every station in which they could any how exist, and that the geometrical tendency to increase must be checked by destruction at some period of life. Our familiarity with the larger domestic animals tends, I think, to mislead us: we see no great destruction falling on them, and we forget that thousands are annually slaughtered for food, and that in a state of nature an equal number would have somehow to be disposed of.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在自然状态下，几乎所有植物都会产生种子，而动物中也很少有不每年配对的。因此，我们可以自信地断言，所有植物和动物都倾向于以几何比例增长，所有植物和动物都会以最快的速度在它们可能存在的每一个站点上储存，并且几何增长的趋势必须通过在生命的某个时期的破坏来抑制。我认为，我们对大型家养动物的熟悉往往会误导我们：我们没有看到它们遭受重大破坏，而且我们忘记了每年有数千只动物被宰杀作为食物，而在自然状态下，同样数量的动物会以某种方式被处理掉。

paragraphs.text: In looking at Nature, it is most necessary to keep the foregoing considerations always in mind—never to forget that every single organic being around us may be said to be striving to the utmost to increase in numbers; that each lives by a struggle at some period of its life; that heavy destruction inevitably falls either on the young or old, during each generation or at recurrent intervals. Lighten any check, mitigate the destruction ever so little, and the number of the species will almost instantaneously increase to any amount. The face of Nature may be compared to a yielding surface, with ten thousand sharp wedges packed close together and driven inwards by incessant blows, sometimes one wedge being struck, and then another with greater force.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在观察自然时，最有必要牢记上述考虑——永远不要忘记，我们周围的每一个有机生物都可以说是在竭尽全力地增加数量；每个人在其一生的某个时期都在奋斗中生活；这种严重的破坏不可避免地降临在年轻人或老年人身上，无论是在每一代还是在周期性的时间间隔内。减轻任何限制，稍微减轻破坏，物种的数量几乎会立即增加到任意数量。大自然的表面可以比作一个屈服的表面，有一万个锋利的楔子紧密地挤在一起，并通过不断的打击向内推进，有时一个楔子被击中，然后另一个楔子受到更大的力量击打。

entries.title: A Morning at the Market

paragraphs.text: Every Saturday morning, my mother and I walk to the market near the river. We bring two big bags and a small list of things to buy. The air is cool, and the street is full of people, bikes, and the smell of fresh bread.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 每个周六早上，我和妈妈步行去河边的市场。我们带上两个大袋子和一张小购物清单。空气凉爽，街上满是行人、自行车和新鲜面包的香味。

paragraphs.text: We always stop at the same fruit stand first. The old man there knows us well, and he often gives me an apple for free. Mother picks the vegetables slowly and asks about the price of fish.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我们总是先在同一个水果摊停下。摊主老爷爷很熟悉我们，常常免费送我一个苹果。妈妈慢慢挑着蔬菜，还问鱼的价格。

paragraphs.text: On the way home, the bags are heavy, but I like to carry them. We talk about what to cook for lunch, and the morning always feels too short.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 回家的路上袋子很重，但我喜欢提着它们。我们聊着午饭做什么，总觉得早晨过得太快。

entries.title: My First Day at a New School

paragraphs.text: Last year my family moved to a new city because of my father's work. I was happy about the bigger house, but I was afraid of one thing: the new school. I did not know anyone there, and I am not good at talking to new people.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 去年因为爸爸的工作，我们全家搬到了一个新城市。我对更大的房子很满意，但有一件事让我害怕：新学校。那里我谁都不认识，而且我不擅长和陌生人说话。

paragraphs.text: On the first morning, I stood at the school gate for a long time. Students walked past me in groups, laughing and talking. I felt very small. Then a tall boy stopped and asked if I was new. He said his name was Daniel and that his family had moved here two years ago, so he knew how I felt.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 第一天早上，我在校门口站了很久。学生们成群结队地走过，又说又笑。我觉得自己很渺小。这时一个高个子男生停下来，问我是不是新来的。他说他叫丹尼尔，两年前他家也搬来这里，所以他懂我的感受。

paragraphs.text: Daniel showed me where my classroom was and sat with me at lunch. In the afternoon, he introduced me to his friends, and one of them lived on my street. By the end of the day, the school did not feel so strange anymore.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 丹尼尔带我找到教室，午饭时还和我坐在一起。下午他把我介绍给他的朋友们，其中一个就住在我家那条街上。一天结束时，学校已经不再那么陌生了。

paragraphs.text: Now, when I see a new student standing alone at the gate, I always walk over and say hello first. I remember how much one small hello can change a whole year.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 现在，每当我看到新同学独自站在校门口，我总会主动走过去打招呼。我记得一句小小的问候能改变整整一年。

entries.title: The Garden Project

paragraphs.text: Our class started a small garden behind the school library last spring. The idea came from our science teacher, Miss Lee. She said that we could learn more from growing real plants than from reading about them in books. At first, many of us thought it would be boring work.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 去年春天，我们班在学校图书馆后面开辟了一个小菜园。这个主意来自我们的科学老师李老师。她说，亲手种植物比在书本上读到的学得更多。起初，我们很多人觉得这会是无聊的活儿。

paragraphs.text: We were wrong. There was so much to do and to decide. Which plants need full sun? How often should we water them? Why did the tomatoes grow well on one side but not on the other? Every week brought a new problem, and we had to find the answers together.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我们错了。要做的事和要做的决定太多了。哪些植物需要充足的阳光？多久浇一次水？为什么西红柿在一边长得好，另一边却不行？每周都有新问题，我们必须一起寻找答案。

paragraphs.text: The hardest time came in July. The weather was very hot, and school was closed for the holiday. We made a plan: every student took two days each month to come and water the garden. Nobody wanted to be the one who let the plants die, so everyone kept their promise.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 最难的时候是七月。天气非常热，学校又放假了。我们订了一个计划：每个学生每月负责两天来浇水。谁都不想成为让植物枯死的那个人，所以每个人都信守了承诺。

paragraphs.text: In September, we picked our first full harvest: tomatoes, beans, and more green vegetables than our class could eat. We sold some at the school gate and gave the rest to the teachers. With the money, we bought seeds for next year.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 九月，我们迎来了第一次大丰收：西红柿、豆角，还有多到全班吃不完的绿叶蔬菜。我们在校门口卖掉了一些，剩下的送给了老师们。用赚来的钱，我们买了来年的种子。

paragraphs.text: Looking back, I think Miss Lee was right, but not only about science. The garden taught us how to share work, how to keep going when things get hard, and how good it feels to build something with your own hands.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 回头看，我觉得李老师说得对，但不仅仅是关于科学。菜园教会了我们如何分担工作，如何在困难时坚持下去，以及亲手创造一样东西的感觉有多好。

entries.title: Why I Stopped Buying Coffee Every Day

paragraphs.text: For three years, I bought a cup of coffee on my way to work every single morning. It was a small pleasure, and I never thought about the cost. Then one evening, I did the math on my phone: the habit was costing me more than a holiday trip every year.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 整整三年，我每天早上上班路上都会买一杯咖啡。这是个小小的享受，我从没想过它的花销。直到一天晚上，我用手机算了一笔账：这个习惯每年花掉的钱比一次度假旅行还多。

paragraphs.text: I did not give up coffee. Instead, I bought a simple machine and started making it at home. The first week felt strange, but soon I actually began to enjoy the quiet ten minutes in my kitchen before leaving the house.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我没有戒掉咖啡，而是买了一台简单的咖啡机，开始在家自己做。第一周感觉很别扭，但很快我竟开始享受出门前在厨房里安静的十分钟。

paragraphs.text: Now I put the money I save into a small account. It is not about the coffee anymore. It is about noticing the small choices that quietly shape a whole year.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 现在我把省下的钱存进一个小账户。这已经不再是咖啡的问题，而是学会留意那些悄悄塑造一整年的小选择。

entries.title: The Lost Phone

paragraphs.text: Last month, I left my phone in a taxi. I realized it only after the car had driven away, and for a moment I just stood on the street, not knowing what to do. My whole life seemed to be in that phone: photos, messages, bank cards, and the numbers of everyone I know.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 上个月，我把手机落在了出租车上。直到车开走我才发现，那一刻我呆站在街头，不知所措。我的整个生活似乎都在那部手机里：照片、信息、银行卡，还有所有认识的人的号码。

paragraphs.text: I borrowed a stranger's phone to call my own number. No one answered the first time, or the second. I was ready to give up when, on the third try, a voice said hello. It was the driver. He had found the phone on the back seat and had already turned around to look for me.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我借了一位路人的手机拨打自己的号码。第一次没人接，第二次也没有。我正准备放弃时，第三次拨打有人应了一声。是那位司机。他在后座发现了手机，并且已经掉头回来找我。

paragraphs.text: Twenty minutes later, he handed it back and refused to take any money. He said that last year someone had returned his daughter's lost bag, and he was simply passing the kindness on. I thanked him again and again, and he just smiled and drove away.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 二十分钟后，他把手机还给我，并拒绝收任何酬谢。他说去年有人把他女儿丢失的包送了回来，他只是把这份善意传递下去。我一再道谢，他只是笑笑，开车走了。

paragraphs.text: That evening I added a new contact to my phone: the driver's number, saved under the name A Friend. The city feels different when you know it is full of people like him.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 那天晚上，我在手机里存了一个新联系人：那位司机的号码，名字存的是“一位朋友”。当你知道城市里满是这样的人时，它给你的感觉都不一样了。

entries.title: Learning to Cook Changed My Life

paragraphs.text: When I moved to the city for my first job, I knew how to cook exactly three things: instant noodles, fried eggs, and rice. For the first few months, I ate out for almost every meal. It was easy and fast, but by the end of each month, my wallet was empty and I felt tired all the time.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 刚到城市开始第一份工作时，我只会做三样东西：泡面、煎蛋和米饭。头几个月，我几乎顿顿在外面吃。这样又方便又快，但每到月底钱包就空了，而且我总觉得疲惫。

paragraphs.text: The change started with a video my sister sent me. It showed a simple tomato soup, made in fifteen minutes with five things from any supermarket. I tried it on a Sunday evening. The kitchen was a mess afterwards, and the soup was too salty, but I had made it myself, and it was warm and real.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 改变始于姐姐发给我的一个视频。视频里是一道简单的番茄汤，用超市随处可买的五样东西，十五分钟就能做好。我在一个周日晚上试了试。事后厨房一片狼藉，汤也太咸了，但那是我亲手做的，温暖而真实。

paragraphs.text: After that, I cooked one new dish every weekend. Some were terrible. I once forgot a chicken in the oven for two hours, and my smoke alarm became famous in the building. But slowly, I built a small list of meals I could make well, and then a bigger one.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 从那以后，我每个周末学做一道新菜。有些做得很糟。有一次我把一只鸡忘在烤箱里两个小时，烟雾报警器在整栋楼都出了名。但慢慢地，我攒下了一小份拿手菜清单，后来清单越来越长。

paragraphs.text: Cooking changed more than my dinner. I started inviting friends over instead of meeting them in noisy restaurants. We talked more and spent less. I began to visit the morning market near my street, where shopping is slower but the food is fresher and the sellers remember your face.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 做饭改变的不只是我的晚餐。我开始邀请朋友来家里，而不是在嘈杂的餐馆见面。我们聊得更多，花得更少。我开始逛家附近的早市，在那里买东西节奏更慢，但食材更新鲜，摊主还会记住你的脸。

paragraphs.text: Two years later, I still keep the first recipe my sister sent me. The paper is marked with oil and sauce, and the soup is still sometimes too salty. But every time I make it, I remember that big changes often begin with one small, simple step.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 两年后的今天，我仍然保留着姐姐发给我的第一份食谱。纸上沾满了油渍和酱汁，汤有时还是会太咸。但每次做这道汤，我都会想起：大的改变往往始于一个小而简单的开始。

entries.title: The Quiet Power of Walking Meetings

paragraphs.text: My manager has an unusual habit: when a discussion needs only two people, she refuses to book a meeting room. Instead, we walk. There is a small park behind our office building, and one slow loop around it takes about twenty minutes, which is exactly the length of a useful conversation.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 我的经理有个不寻常的习惯：只需要两个人讨论的事情，她从不订会议室，而是带你去散步。我们办公楼后面有个小公园，慢慢绕一圈大约二十分钟，恰好是一次有效谈话的长度。
